---
title: "Sampling"
author: "nbanda25"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(primer.data)

truth <- mean(shaming$age, na.rm = TRUE)
```


A census measures every unit in a population — and it is usually too expensive, and often impossible: any population that reaches into the future can never be fully counted. So almost all of data science works instead from a sample and reasons back to the population. How much we can trust that reasoning is the subject of this tutorial.

```{r}
#| cache: true
set.seed(10)
samples <- tibble(trial = 1:1000) |>
  mutate(
    sample      = map(trial, ~ shaming |> slice_sample(n = 50)),
    sample_mean = map_dbl(sample, ~ mean(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE)),
    sample_se   = map_dbl(sample, ~ sd(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(50)),
    lower       = sample_mean - 1.96 * sample_se,
    upper       = sample_mean + 1.96 * sample_se
  ) |>
  select(-sample)
```

```{r}
samples |>
  summarise(coverage = mean(lower < truth & upper > truth))
```

```{r}
ggplot(samples |> slice(1:100), aes(y = trial)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper),
                 height = 0.3, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(x = sample_mean), size = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = truth, color = "red",
             linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "100 unbiased samples of 50 voters each",
       subtitle = "About 95% of the intervals contain the truth",
       x = "Age (years)",
       y = "Sample number",
       caption = "Source: Gerber, Green, Larimer 2008 via primer.data::shaming") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
set.seed(10)
biased_sample <- shaming |>
  mutate(wt = if_else(age > 40, 3, 1)) |>
  slice_sample(n = 50, weight_by = wt)

set.seed(11)
unbiased_sample <- shaming |> slice_sample(n = 50)

bind_rows(
  biased_sample   |> mutate(origin = "Biased (age > 40 weighted 3x)"),
  unbiased_sample |> mutate(origin = "Unbiased")
) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "grey50", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~ origin) +
  labs(title = "Age distribution: biased vs. unbiased samples of 50",
       subtitle = "The biased sample is visibly skewed toward older voters",
       x = "Age (years)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
```
